title: ModelEditorData
description: Flat DTO for the model editor page. Mixers are included as a full 64-row list (the UI filters empty ones client-side) since that's ~10 KB of JSON — well under the Tauri IPC threshold.

active.description: Whether this slot is listed as "active" in `RADIO/models.txt` (i.e. selectable from the radio's model list at runtime).

curves.description: Up to 32 curves referenced by mixers and expos (`curve_type > 0` ⇒ referenced by id; `curve_value` is the point array offset).

custom_functions.description: Up to 64 "Special Functions" — switch-driven actions such as playing a sound, resetting a timer, adjusting a GVar, overriding a channel, etc.

expos.description: Input expos — shape raw stick/pot deflection into the values that the mixer sees. Up to 64 rows.

filename.description: Filename of the model on disk, typically `modelN.bin`.

flight_modes.description: Up to 9 flight modes. `flight_modes[0]` is the default mode (always active when no other FM's switch is); 1..=8 activate by switch.

limits.description: Per-output-channel clamps, offsets, inversion and optional curve. One row per channel (CH1..CH16+).

logical_switches.description: 64 logical switches (L1..L64). A logical switch is a boolean computed from other switches/sources/sensors/timers; it can then be used anywhere a real switch can.

mixers.description: Full 64-row mixer table. Each row combines a source (stick, switch, channel, ...) into a destination channel with weight/offset/curve. Rows with `src_raw == 0` are empty/unused.

name.description: Model name shown on the TX screen. Up to 10 characters (zchar-encoded in the binary; plain UTF-8 here).

slot.description: Zero-based index of this model's slot on the SD card (0 = first).

telemetry_sensors.description: Up to 60 telemetry sensors streamed by the RX/receiver. Most fields are populated by the firmware at runtime; only `label` is user-editable.

timers.description: The three user-configurable timers. `timers[0]` is Timer 1 on the radio.

CurveEditData.description: One curve definition referenced by mixers and expos. Curve points live in a shared point buffer on-disk; editing individual points is not yet wired up in the UI — only the metadata (type, smooth, name, point count) is.

index.description: Zero-based curve index (0..=31). Reference this id from `MixerEditData.curve_value` etc.

points_minus_5.description: Number of points minus 5 (firmware convention). So 0 = 5 points, 8 = 13 points, etc. Signed 6-bit.

CustomFunctionEditData.description: One `CustomFunctionData` entry — the "Special Functions" tab. Binds a switch condition to an action (play sound, reset timer, adjust a GVar, override a channel, etc). SF1 = `index 0`, SF64 = `index 63`.

active.description: Enable flag (1 bit). Only meaningful for functions below `FUNC_FIRST_WITHOUT_ENABLE` (= 10). For higher indices the firmware repurposes this byte (e.g. repeat counter for play functions).

all_mode.description: `all` variant — `mode` byte (secondary parameter, e.g. GVar adjust mode: constant/incr/source).

all_param.description: `all` variant — `param` byte (tertiary parameter, e.g. GVar id, timer id).

all_spare.description: `all` variant — spare 32-bit slot reserved for future expansions.

all_val.description: `all` variant — numeric operand `val`. For SET_TIMER it's the seconds; for ADJUST_GVAR it's the value; for OVERRIDE_CHANNEL the channel value.

func.description: Action id — see `FUNC_*` constants. A few common ones on Tango: 0 OVERRIDE_CHANNEL, 3 RESET, 4 SET_TIMER, 5 ADJUST_GVAR, 10 PLAY_SOUND, 11 PLAY_TRACK, 14 PLAY_SCRIPT, 16 BACKGND_MUSIC.

payload_raw.description: Raw 8-byte union payload — the source of truth for variants the editor doesn't decode explicitly yet. Preserved across save so custom variants aren't zeroed out on round-trip.

play_name.description: Sound / script filename for PLAY_TRACK / PLAY_SCRIPT / BACKGND_MUSIC. Up to 6 ASCII chars (no extension); resolved at runtime as `SOUNDS/<lang>/<name>.wav` etc.

switch.description: Signed 9-bit switch reference (SWSRC). The function activates when this switch is true. 0 = disabled.

ExpoEditData.description: One `ExpoData` row — the "Inputs" tab in the OpenTX companion. Expos shape how a raw stick / pot deflection is mapped to the input that the mixer later reads. Typical use: add exponential curves to the sticks.

carry_trim.description: Trim handling: signed 6-bit. Negative values disable trim from this source; 0 = use the default trim for the stick.

curve_type.description: Curve type: 0 = standard, >0 = custom curve id.

curve_value.description: Curve amount (for standard) or curve id (for custom).

flight_modes.description: Flight-mode bitmask — a bit set here disables the expo in that mode.

index.description: Row position in the expo table (0..=63).

mode.description: Activation mode (2 bits): 0 = disabled, 1 = positive-only, 2 = negative-only, 3 = both halves of the stick active.

scale.description: Input scale factor (14 bits). Rarely changed — use `weight` for normal gain adjustments.

src_raw.description: Raw mix-source index used as the input (usually a stick or pot).

switch.description: Signed 9-bit switch reference — expo is only active when this switch is ON. 0 = always active.

FlightModeEditData.description: One `FlightModeData` entry. FM0 (index 0) is the default — always active unless another FM's switch supersedes it. FM1..FM8 activate when their `switch` becomes true.

fade_in.description: Fade-in time when this FM becomes active, in tenths of a second.

fade_out.description: Fade-out time when leaving this FM, in tenths of a second.

gvars.description: Values for the 9 global variables (GVars 1..9) while this FM is active. `gvars[i]` is the raw signed value for GVar i+1.

name.description: Optional 6-character FM label shown on the TX screen.

switch.description: Signed 9-bit switch reference that activates this FM. Unused for FM0.

curve.description: Optional output curve applied after mixing, as a signed id into the curve table. 0 = straight-through.

min.description: Lower clamp on the output, as a signed 11-bit value (-1024 = -100%).

ppm_center.description: Sub-trim: PPM pulse-width offset around the 1500 µs centre, in µs. Range roughly -500..=500.

revert.description: 1 = invert output polarity for this channel.

symetrical.description: 1 = symmetrical trim (trim centres between `min` and `max` rather than 0). 0 = asymmetric.

LogicalSwitchEditData.description: One `LogicalSwitchData` entry — a derived boolean switch that can be used anywhere a real switch can. L1 = `index 0`, L64 = `index 63`.

andsw.description: Optional extra AND/OR switch gating the final result.

andsw_type.description: How `andsw` combines with the primary condition: 0 = AND, 1 = OR.

delay.description: Activation delay in tenths of a second after the condition becomes true.

duration.description: How long the switch stays ON after activation, in tenths of seconds. 0 = indefinite (stays true as long as the condition holds).

func.description: Operator / function — see `LS_FUNC_*` constants. Broad groups: 0 = off; 1..=7 compare a source to a constant (`V<`, `V>`, range, ...); 8..=10,18 boolean ops (AND/OR/XOR/STICKY); 11 EDGE; 12..=14 compare two sources (`a == b` / `a > b` / `a < b`); 15..=16 DIFF functions; 17 TIMER.

v1.description: First operand. Interpretation depends on `func`: mix source (compares), switch source (AND/OR/EDGE), or integer (TIMER seconds).

v2.description: Second operand. Typically a constant value for threshold compares, a switch for boolean ops, a source id for two-source compares, or a time for EDGE / TIMER.

v3.description: Third operand — only used by EDGE (`time_max`) and DIFF (threshold). Zero for other functions.

carry_trim.description: If non-zero, exclude the source's trim contribution from this row.

curve_type.description: Curve type: 0 = standard (differential/expo curve), >0 = custom curve selected by `curve_value`.

curve_value.description: Meaning depends on `curve_type`: for standard, the curve amount (expo percent); for custom, the signed curve id referencing `curves[]`.

delay_down.description: Fall-time delay, in tenths of a second.

delay_up.description: Rise-time delay before the output follows the input, in tenths of a second. 0 = instant.

flight_modes.description: Flight-mode bitmask — a bit set here DISABLES the row when that flight mode is active. 0 = always active regardless of flight mode.

index.description: Row position in the mixer table (0..=63).

mltpx.description: How this row combines with earlier rows targeting the same channel: 0 = add (`+=`), 1 = multiply (`*=`), 2 = replace (`:=`).

name.description: Optional 6-character label displayed in the mixer list.

offset.description: Constant added after the weight is applied, as a signed 14-bit value mapped to percent (-8192 = -100%).

speed_down.description: Fall-rate limit, in tenths of a second per unit.

speed_up.description: Rise-rate limit (slew) in tenths of a second per unit. 0 = instant.

src_label.description: Resolved human-readable label for `src_raw` (e.g. "Rud", "CH1", "L3"). Computed by the backend; round-trip ignores this field on import.

src_raw.description: Raw source index — sticks (Rud/Thr/Ele/Ail), pots, switches, channels, logical switches, telemetry sensors, etc. Use `src_label` for the human-readable name.

switch.description: Signed 9-bit switch reference (SWSRC). The row is only active while this switch is ON. 0 = always active.

weight.description: Weight applied to the source, as a signed 11-bit value mapped to percent: -1024 = -100%, 0 = 0%, 1023 = +100%. Full range -1024..=1023.

TelemetrySensorEditData.description: One telemetry sensor — usually auto-populated by the receiver over CRSF. Only `label` is routinely user-edited; the other fields are read-mostly.

filter.description: 1 = apply the firmware's low-pass filter before display.

id.description: Physical/logical sensor id (CRSF type code or protocol-specific id).

instance.description: Sensor instance — lets multiple sensors of the same type coexist.

label.description: User-facing sensor label, up to 4 characters (zchar on-disk).

logs.description: 1 = include this sensor in SD telemetry logs.

persistent.description: 1 = save last value across power cycles.

prec.description: Number of decimal places when displaying this sensor (0..=3).

sensor_type.description: 0 = custom (streamed from RX), 1 = calculated (derived from other sensors on-radio).

sub_id.description: Sub-id of the physical sensor (firmware internal).

unit.description: 6-bit unit code (see FreedomTX `TelemetryUnit` enum — volts, amps, celsius, feet, metres, knots, rpm, ...).

countdown_beep.description: Countdown notification style: 0 = off, 1 = beep, 2 = voice, 3 = haptic.

minute_beep.description: If non-zero, emit a beep every minute of the timer.

mode.description: Timer mode / source. 0 = off; positive values reference a switch or channel that runs the timer while ON; negative = inverted.

name.description: Optional 3-character label shown on the timer bar.

persistent.description: Persistence: 0 = don't persist, 1 = per-flight (reset on "new flight"), 2 = per-model (saved across power cycles).

start.description: Starting value in seconds. Used as the countdown start for countdown timers; ignored (stays at 0) for stopwatch mode.
